nakshatras.name: Ashwini
nakshatras.career: Medicine, healing arts, horse-related fields, speed sports, emergency services
nakshatras.spiritual: Ashwini represents the dawn of consciousness. The Ashwini Kumaras are divine physicians; healing others is the highest calling of this nakshatra.

nakshatras.name: Bharani
nakshatras.career: Entertainment, creative arts, occult sciences, medicine, law
nakshatras.spiritual: Bharani holds the power of apabharani—taking away. It teaches that creation requires destruction. Confronting mortality brings deep liberation.

nakshatras.name: Krittika
nakshatras.career: Military, cooking, metallurgy, surgery, teaching, leadership roles
nakshatras.spiritual: Krittika's fire cuts through illusion. Tapas (austerity) and Agni puja (fire worship) are powerful practices. This nakshatra bestows the power to burn away karmic obstacles.

nakshatras.name: Rohini
nakshatras.career: Agriculture, arts, luxury goods, music, beauty industry, real estate
nakshatras.spiritual: Rohini is the most beloved nakshatra of the Moon. Its power is Rohana Shakti—the power of growth. Beauty and abundance are spiritual gifts when used mindfully.

nakshatras.name: Mrigashira
nakshatras.career: Research, travel, media, writing, wildlife, textile industry
nakshatras.spiritual: Mrigashira represents the eternal search for divine nectar. The deer's quest symbolizes the soul seeking moksha. Meditation calms this nakshatra's restless seeking.

nakshatras.name: Ardra
nakshatras.career: Research, technology, psychology, meteorology, social work
nakshatras.spiritual: Ardra's storm clears what is stagnant. Rudra is the fierce aspect of Shiva who tears away attachment. Shadow work and emotional release are profound practices.

nakshatras.name: Punarvasu
nakshatras.career: Education, philosophy, counseling, architecture, publishing, travel
nakshatras.spiritual: Punarvasu means 'return of light.' Aditi is boundless abundance. This nakshatra teaches that every ending is a new beginning. Gratitude practices are transformative.

nakshatras.name: Pushya
nakshatras.career: Teaching, nursing, social work, dairy industry, religious work, management
nakshatras.spiritual: Pushya is considered the most auspicious nakshatra. Brihaspati bestows divine grace. This nakshatra is ideal for starting spiritual practices and receiving initiation.

nakshatras.name: Ashlesha
nakshatras.career: Psychology, occult sciences, astrology, medicine, diplomacy
nakshatras.spiritual: The serpent represents Kundalini energy. Ashlesha natives are called to awaken this dormant power through yoga, meditation, and Naga puja.

nakshatras.name: Magha
nakshatras.career: Government, leadership, entertainment, history, traditional arts
nakshatras.spiritual: Magha calls for honoring ancestors (Pitru Tarpan). The throne symbolizes righteous authority. Ketu's influence makes this a deeply karmic nakshatra connected to past lives.

nakshatras.name: Purva Phalguni
nakshatras.career: Entertainment, arts, luxury, fashion, beauty, romantic professions
nakshatras.spiritual: Bhaga bestows enjoyment of life's pleasures as divine gift. This nakshatra teaches that beauty and pleasure, enjoyed without attachment, can be pathways to the divine.

nakshatras.name: Uttara Phalguni
nakshatras.career: Social work, management, HR, marriage counseling, service industries
nakshatras.spiritual: Aryaman governs sacred contracts and commitments. This nakshatra teaches that service and responsibility, when performed with joy, become yoga.

nakshatras.name: Hasta
nakshatras.career: Craftsmanship, surgery, writing, healing arts, massage, mechanical skills
nakshatras.spiritual: Hasta means 'hand'—the power of manifestation through skill. Mudra practice and hands-on healing arts are powerful spiritual practices for this nakshatra.

nakshatras.name: Chitra
nakshatras.career: Architecture, design, jewelry, fashion, creative arts, engineering
nakshatras.spiritual: Vishvakarma is the divine craftsman. Chitra teaches that creation itself is a spiritual act. Beauty crafted with intention becomes an offering to the divine.

nakshatras.name: Swati
nakshatras.career: Business, trading, music, travel, law, diplomacy
nakshatras.spiritual: Swati is like a young plant bending in the wind—flexible without breaking. Vayu teaches Pranayama as a direct path to controlling the mind.

nakshatras.name: Vishakha
nakshatras.deity: Indra and Agni (king of gods and fire god)
nakshatras.career: Politics, research, religious leadership, competitive sports, commerce
nakshatras.spiritual: Vishakha means 'forked' or 'branched'—representing the moment of choice between dharma and adharma. Indra and Agni together bring purification through trials.

nakshatras.name: Anuradha
nakshatras.career: Research, occult sciences, management, international relations, counseling
nakshatras.spiritual: Mitra governs sacred friendship and alliances. Anuradha teaches that devotion (bhakti) to a teacher or deity opens the deepest spiritual doors.

nakshatras.name: Jyeshtha
nakshatras.career: Leadership, military, occult, politics, protection services
nakshatras.spiritual: Jyeshtha means 'eldest' or 'most senior.' Indra's blessing comes to those who protect the vulnerable. This nakshatra is called to leadership in dharmic causes.

nakshatras.name: Mula
nakshatras.career: Research, philosophy, medicine, detective work, occult sciences
nakshatras.spiritual: Mula means 'root.' Nirriti dissolves what has outlived its purpose. This nakshatra demands going to the root of existence—profound spiritual investigation.

nakshatras.name: Purva Ashadha
nakshatras.career: Law, diplomacy, water-related industries, shipping, social reform
nakshatras.spiritual: Apas purifies and nourishes. This nakshatra carries the power of invigoration. Water-based spiritual practices—sacred bathing, river worship, Ganga puja—are potent.

nakshatras.name: Uttara Ashadha
nakshatras.career: Government, leadership, military, education, administration
nakshatras.spiritual: The Vishvadevas represent all virtues combined. This nakshatra pursues ultimate dharma. Victory through righteousness—Uttara Ashadha teaches that principled living is spiritual practice.

nakshatras.name: Shravana
nakshatras.career: Education, counseling, media, religion, social service, management
nakshatras.spiritual: Shravana means 'hearing.' Vishnu preserves through sacred knowledge. Sravana—listening to scripture and spiritual discourse—is the primary practice of this nakshatra.

nakshatras.name: Dhanishtha
nakshatras.career: Music, dance, real estate, administration, athletics, sciences
nakshatras.spiritual: The Vasus bring elemental abundance. Dhanishtha's power is Khyapayitri Shakti—the power to bring fame and abundance. Nada yoga (sound as spiritual practice) is ideal.

nakshatras.name: Shatabhisha
nakshatras.deity: Varuna (god of cosmic order and water)
nakshatras.career: Healing arts, research, astrology, mysticism, technology, astronomy
nakshatras.spiritual: Varuna governs cosmic law and the mysteries of water. Shatabhisha carries healing power. Solitary retreat, contemplation, and mastery of esoteric knowledge are the spiritual path.

nakshatras.name: Purva Bhadrapada
nakshatras.symbol: Sword, front legs of funeral cot
nakshatras.career: Research, occult sciences, philosophy, finance, revolution
nakshatras.spiritual: Aja Ekapada represents the lightning flash of transformation. This nakshatra is called to radical inner transformation—often through intense practices and spiritual crisis.

nakshatras.name: Uttara Bhadrapada
nakshatras.symbol: Back legs of funeral cot, twins
nakshatras.career: Social work, counseling, teaching, research, medicine, spiritual guidance
nakshatras.spiritual: Ahir Budhnya is the dragon of the deep waters—unconscious wisdom. This nakshatra completes karmic cycles and prepares the soul for liberation. Deep meditation and service to humanity.

nakshatras.name: Revati
nakshatras.career: Spiritual work, arts, marine industry, travel, social work, healing
nakshatras.spiritual: Revati is the last nakshatra—the completion of the cosmic cycle. Pushan guides souls home. This nakshatra holds the gift of spiritual wholeness; its path is unconditional compassion.
